summary: Huang Zhong; the chapter's hero. At Dingjun Mountain, on Fa Zheng's signal - a red flag dropped from the heights - charges down on Xiahou Yuan's resting troops and cuts him in half from shoulder to waist in a single blow. The killing of Xiahou Yuan that breaks Wei's hold on Hanzhong.

Fa Zheng; on Mount Dingjun's heights signalling Huang Zhong with white and red flags - white to wait, red to charge. Times the strike for when Xiahou Yuan's men have collapsed exhausted in the noon heat.

Xiahou Yuan; defends Dingjun Mountain against Huang Zhong. Ignores Zhang He's warnings about Fa Zheng's positioning, lets his men rest at midday, gets surprised by the charge; cut in two by Huang Zhong. Cao Cao loses one of his most senior generals.
Zhang He; Xiahou Yuan's deputy at Dingjun; warned of the trap but couldn't stop it; falls back after Xiahou Yuan dies.
Xiahou Shang; with Xiahou Yuan at Dingjun.
Han Hao; killed at Tiandang Mountain - covered in passing here in the Dingjun aftermath.
Cao Cao; learns of Xiahou Yuan's death at Dingjun; vows revenge; advances with the main Wei army into Hanzhong to face Liu Bei directly.
Liu Bei; on campaign in Hanzhong; receives the Dingjun victory; advances his army into the mountains to seize the rest of the territory.
Zhuge Liang; directs the whole Hanzhong campaign by remote management - sends Huang Zhong's age-baiting plan, sends Fa Zheng to plant the flags. Spell-perfect.

Zhao Yun; later in the chapter - the one-man stand at the granary. Cuts a path through the Wei encirclement to rescue Huang Zhong; reaches camp; opens his gates wide, sits silent on horse - Cao Cao thinks ambush and pulls back; then crushes the retreating Wei from behind. Liu Bei famously says: 'Zilong is all guts.'
Zhang Fei; raids Wei's grain stores.
Wei Yan; in the Hanzhong fighting.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Ma Chao; with Liu Bei's Hanzhong forces.
Xiahou Dun; with Cao Cao.
Xu Huang; with Cao Cao in Hanzhong.
Cao Hong; with Cao Cao in Hanzhong.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Cao Xiu; with Cao Cao.
Liu Ye; advises Cao Cao to attack Yi Province while Hanzhong is in flux - Cao Cao demurs.
Liu Feng; on the Hanzhong front.
Meng Da; on the Hanzhong front.
Meng Da (duplicate record).
Chen Shi; Liu Bei's officer in the Hanzhong campaign; captured then exchanged for Xiahou Shang.
Chen Shi (duplicate record).
Yan Yan; with Huang Zhong at Dingjun as his deputy - fights well in the climactic charge.
Yan Yan (duplicate record).
Wang Ping; on the Wei side here, with Xu Huang against Huang Zhong; defects to Liu Bei after Xu Huang's debacle at the Han River - the start of Wang Ping's long Shu career.
Lady Cao; named in passing.
Guan Lu; named in passing.
Du Xi; named in passing.
Cai Yan; named in passing in the chapter's literary frame.
Cai Yong; named in passing.
Handan Chun; named in passing.
Yang Xiu; in attendance with Cao Cao.
Yang Xiu (duplicate record).
Wei Zhongdao; named in passing.
Wen Pin; with Cao Cao.
Zhang Liang; named in passing (historical Han-founding strategist as literary allusion).
Zhang Liang (duplicate record).
Zhang Zhu; minor figure named in passing.
Dong Si; minor figure named in passing.
Dong Si (duplicate record).
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Ni; minor Shu officer named in passing.
Zhang Yi; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).
Zhang Yi (duplicate record).

Cao Cao; the chapter's Wei centerpiece. Stuck in the 'chicken-ribs' (jīle) standoff on the Han River - neither swallow nor spit. Yang Xiu reads the watchword and tells the men to pack for retreat; Cao Cao executes him on a treason pretext, but soon does retreat. Loses Hanzhong; loses face.
Liu Bei; secures Hanzhong; his army's morale soars. Will be proclaimed King of Hanzhong in the next chapter.
Zhuge Liang; directs the Han River campaign; Zhao Yun-and-empty-camp routine spooks Cao Cao's army into rout.
Zhao Yun; the empty-camp gambit at the Han River - opens gates and stands silent; Cao Cao bolts; the resulting Wei rout costs Cao Cao the Han River line.

Yang Xiu; reads Cao Cao's 'chicken-ribs' watchword and tells the camp to pack - Cao Cao executes him for breaking discipline. The famous 'too clever' death scene; Yang Xiu's previous over-readings of Cao Cao (the box of biscuits, the garden gate, the bowl of cake) are recapped in retrospect.
Yang Xiu (duplicate record).
Xu Huang; commands a wing at the Han River for Cao Cao; routed in the empty-camp panic.
Wu Lan; Liu Bei's officer; killed in skirmishing with Wei.
Huang Zhong; with Liu Bei at the Han River.
Zhang Fei; with Liu Bei.
Ma Chao; with Liu Bei.
Wei Yan; with Liu Bei - shoots Cao Cao in the mouth with an arrow, knocking out two of Cao Cao's teeth, before Cao Cao is rescued.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Xiahou Dun; with Cao Cao.
Xu Chu; defends Cao Cao after the arrow.
Pang De; with Cao Cao on the Han River.
Cao Pi; remains at the Wei court; named here as Cao Cao's elder son.
Cao Zhang; named in passing.
Cao Zhi; the literary son; the Yang Xiu episode is colored by Cao Zhi's faction at court - Yang Xiu had been backing Cao Zhi's bid against Cao Pi.
Wu Zhi; one of Cao Pi's advisors; coaches him through the succession intrigue against Cao Zhi.
Liu Feng; on the Hanzhong front.
Meng Da; on the Hanzhong front.
Meng Da (duplicate record).
Shentu Pan; named in passing.
Wang Ping; with Liu Bei on the Han River.
Yan Yan; with Huang Zhong.
Yan Yan (duplicate record).
Liu Bei; proclaimed King of Hanzhong by his officers - the formal break from purely-Han pretense. Tries to refuse three times; takes the throne under pressure; gives Liu Shan the heir's title.

Zhuge Liang; orchestrates the kingship petition; lays out the Five Tiger Generals - Guan Yu, Zhang Fei, Zhao Yun, Ma Chao, Huang Zhong. Then orders Guan Yu north to capture Fancheng - the war that will kill him.

Guan Yu; appointed Marquis of Hanshouting and ordered north to attack Cao Ren at Fancheng. Initially insulted at being grouped with the 'old soldier' Huang Zhong; placated by Fei Shi. Departs Jingzhou for the Fan campaign - the road that ends at Maicheng.
Huang Zhong; one of the Five Tiger Generals. Sends Fei Shi to placate Guan Yu's wounded pride.
Zhang Fei; one of the Five Tiger Generals.
Zhao Yun; one of the Five Tiger Generals.
Ma Chao; one of the Five Tiger Generals.
Liu Shan; named heir to the kingship of Hanzhong; the first time he is foregrounded as the next Liu Bei.
Fei Shi; sent to soothe Guan Yu's pride at being grouped with Huang Zhong; talks him into accepting the appointment.

Cao Cao; furious at Liu Bei's kingship - vows to lead an army to crush him; Sima Yi suggests instead inciting Sun Quan to break the Wu-Shu alliance and attack Jingzhou. The plot that will kill Guan Yu begins here.
Sima Yi; first major counsel - proposes goading Sun Quan to attack Jingzhou to relieve Wei pressure. The strategy that produces the Lu Meng raid.
Sun Quan; receives Cao Cao's envoy proposing alliance against Liu Bei; begins to take the bait.

Zhuge Jin; sent to Jingzhou to propose a marriage between Sun Quan's son and Guan Yu's daughter - Guan Yu refuses with the famous line 'My tiger daughter shall not be wed to a dog's whelp'; the insult turns Sun Quan to war.
Guan Ping; with Guan Yu - present when Zhuge Jin makes the marriage proposal.
Cao Ren; commands the Fancheng garrison about to be attacked by Guan Yu.
Yu Jin; named in passing - will soon be sent as Cao Cao's relief commander to Fan.
Zhang Zhao; on Sun Quan's council; advocates the deal with Wei.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Gu Yong; on Sun Quan's council.
Lu Jing; on Sun Quan's council.
Ma Liang; serves with Guan Yu at Jingzhou; will go north with him.
Fa Zheng; on Liu Bei's council in Chengdu - endorses the kingship.
Wang Fu; in Guan Yu's Jingzhou council; will figure later in the Maicheng disaster.
Mi Fang; in Jingzhou under Guan Yu - left as garrison while Guan Yu marches north; the future betrayer.
Liao Hua; with Guan Yu's force.
Liu Feng; in the Liu Bei court structure.
Meng Da; in the Liu Bei court structure.
Meng Da (duplicate record).
Pan Jun; on Guan Yu's staff at Jingzhou.
Shen Dan; in Liu Bei's court.
Shentu Pan; in Liu Bei's court.
Wang Ping; in the Liu Bei ranks.
Wei Yan; in the Liu Bei ranks.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Xu Jing; minor official; petitions for the kingship.
Yi Ji; named in passing in Liu Bei's court.
Man Chong; named in passing on the Wei side.
Bu Zhi; named in passing in Sun Quan's court.
Zhao Lei; minor figure named in passing.

Guan Yu; the chapter's centerpiece. Faces Pang De's challenge at Fan - Pang De rides out with a coffin sworn to fight to the death; the two duel a hundred bouts to a draw; on the second day Pang De wounds Guan Yu in the left arm with a feathered arrow. Then floods the Wei relief army by breaking the Xiang River dams - drowns the Seven Armies; captures Yu Jin; beheads Pang De who refuses to surrender. The peak of Guan Yu's career.

Pang De; the standout Wei figure. Marches out from Fan with a coffin behind him sworn either to kill Guan Yu or be killed; wounds Guan Yu's arm with an arrow; trapped on a hilltop when Guan Yu floods the river; captured; refuses to surrender; beheaded standing. His martyrdom canonised by Cao Cao.

Yu Jin; Cao Cao's senior general sent with Pang De to relieve Fan. Camps the seven armies in a low valley despite warnings; floods drown his force; surrenders to Guan Yu and is taken prisoner. His career is over; he reappears once more as a ruined man in Cao Pi's day.

Cao Cao; learns of the disaster at Fan - the loss of the Seven Armies under Yu Jin and Pang De's execution. Considers fleeing the capital; Sima Yi talks him down and proposes the Sun Quan back-channel that produces the Lu Meng raid.
Liu Bei; off-stage; the news of Guan Yu's flood-victory at Fan reaches Chengdu.
Cao Ren; trapped inside Fan with Cao Cao's brother-in-arms officers; the city surrounded by floodwater after Guan Yu's dam-break.
Guan Ping; with Guan Yu at Fan; participates in the flood-attack; binds Yu Jin and Pang De when they are taken.
Guan Xing; reaches his father at Fan with despatches from Liu Bei.
Ma Chao; named in passing.
Man Chong; Cao Ren's chief advisor in Fan; urges him to hold the city through the flood.
Liao Hua; with Guan Yu at Fan.
Jia Xu; on Cao Cao's council; advises during the Fan crisis.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Cheng He; Yu Jin's officer; killed in the flood disaster at Fan.
Dong Chao; killed in the flood at Fan.
Dong Chao (duplicate record).
Dong Heng; killed in the flood at Fan.
Pang Hui; Pang De's son - Cao Cao raises him after Pang De's martyrdom; figures decades later in the fall of Shu.
Pang Rou; Pang De's wife - Cao Cao confers a stipend on the bereaved family.

Guan Yu; takes a poisoned arrow in the shoulder during the Fancheng siege; Hua Tuo arrives by river to treat him. The famous board-game scene: Guan Yu plays go with Ma Liang, sleeve rolled up, while Hua Tuo scrapes the bone to remove the poison - no flinch. Then forces the siege of Fan despite the wound. By chapter's end, Lu Meng has begun his counter-stroke in white robes against Jingzhou; the noose tightens.

Hua Tuo; arrives at Guan Yu's camp from across the river and operates on the poisoned arm - scrapes the bone clean with no anaesthetic; admires Guan Yu's iron will. The novel's most famous surgery scene.
Ma Liang; plays go with Guan Yu during Hua Tuo's surgery - sits steady as the bone is scraped.

Lu Xun; the chapter's strategic centerpiece. Lu Meng feigns illness; Sun Quan appoints Lu Xun in his place; Lu Xun writes a fawning letter to Guan Yu praising his greatness - Guan Yu drops his guard, transfers half his Jingzhou garrison north to Fan. The deception that opens the back door.
Lu Su; named in passing in the recap of the Wu-Shu alliance.
Sun Quan; gives Lu Meng the secret command to take Jingzhou; ratifies the Lu Xun feint.
Jiang Ji; on Cao Cao's council during the Fan crisis.
Sima Yi; on Cao Cao's council; with Jiang Ji presses the Sun Quan back-channel - let Wu attack Jingzhou.
Cao Cao; commits to the Wu alliance against Liu Bei; sends Xu Huang to relieve Fan.
Xu Huang; sent by Cao Cao to relieve Fan; gathers a fresh army; will engage Guan Yu next chapter.
Cao Ren; still besieged inside Fan; the floods are receding; awaits Xu Huang's relief.
Pang De; named in passing in the recap of the Fan disaster.
Yu Jin; languishing as a Wu prisoner.
Guan Ping; with Guan Yu at Fan during the surgery and through the siege.
Wang Fu; warns Guan Yu about Lu Meng's deception; Guan Yu doesn't act in time.
Mi Fang; left to garrison Jingzhou - drinking heavily, lax discipline; will let Lu Meng's white-robed merchants slip through.
Liang Da; minor Wu officer named in passing.
Lu Jing; in Sun Quan's council.
Jiang Qin; with Lu Meng's force preparing the Jingzhou strike.
Cheng Pu; named in passing - already dead; the old generation.
Ding Feng; with the Wu army.
Ding Feng (duplicate record).
Han Dang; with the Wu army.
Pan Zhang; with Lu Meng's force.
Sun Jiao; with the Wu army.
Sun Jing; with the Wu army.
Xu Sheng; with the Wu army.
Yu Fan; on Sun Quan's council.
Zhang Zhao; on Sun Quan's council.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Zhou Tai; with the Wu army.
Zhou Tai (duplicate record).
Zhou Yu; named in passing - already dead; the old generation.
Zhu Ran; with the Wu army; will figure in the Maicheng pursuit.
Pang De; named in passing in the recap of the Fan disaster - already dead by execution last chapter.
